reasoning: Programmatic-only: 4 major concern(s): Table split across page break(s): 6 table(s) with repeated headers across consecutive pages; Table caption coded as header row: 1 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title; Data row marked as header: 4 table(s) have numeric/data values in the header row

concerns.description: Table split across page break(s): 6 table(s) with repeated headers across consecutive pages
concerns.analysis: split_table_count=6. Examples: pages 5-6: 6 cols (, 2010, 2011...); pages 7-8: 6 cols (county, 2010, 2011...); pages 10-11: 6 cols (state, 2010, 2011...). Tables spanning multiple pages were extracted as separate table elements with duplicated header rows. The HTML output will show multiple tables instead of one continuous table — a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 1 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis: caption_as_header=1. Examples: p2: "SUMMARY". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

concerns.description: Data row marked as header: 4 table(s) have numeric/data values in the header row
concerns.analysis: data_as_header=4. Examples: p5: [2010, 2011, 2012]; p6: [2010, 2011, 2012]; p9: [2010, 2011, 2012]. Header rows should contain column labels, not data values.

concerns.description: Footnote(s) converted to bulleted list: 1 unordered list(s) after table(s) appear to be footnotes
concerns.analysis: footnote_as_list_count=1. Examples: 1 item(s): "DSS - Department of Social Services issues free su". Table footnotes (often marked with *, †, ‡) were converted to <ul><li> elements instead of paragraphs. This changes the semantic meaning — screen readers announce them as list items rather than explanatory notes.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 16 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=16. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.

action_items: Table split across page break(s): 6 table(s) with repeated headers across consecutive pages
Table caption coded as header row: 1 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
Data row marked as header: 4 table(s) have numeric/data values in the header row
Footnote(s) converted to bulleted list: 1 unordered list(s) after table(s) appear to be footnotes
